mechanism_narrative: A persistently low and stable federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity present values and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following months with below-median FEDFUNDS are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or mean return is lower than unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over a three-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede earnings downgrades and risk-off repositioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when the trigger fires over a walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous elevation in the federal funds rate and consumer price inflation creates a restrictive real policy stance that compresses profit margins and valuations; the joint effect produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Bivariate rank correlation between the interaction term and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recoveries, quarter-over-quarter acceleration in real GDP growth signals strengthening aggregate demand that lifts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In quarters classified as post-recession recovery, GDPC1 growth acceleration fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with Rank IC > 0.04 over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate a contracting manufacturing sector that reduces aggregate output and earnings visibility, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following consecutive INDPRO declines are not negative in at least 55% of triggered instances during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Month-over-month changes in PAYEMS show a rank correlation below 0.03 with 20-day SPX returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1,400 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and related downstream demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as growth expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1400, 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales confirm that labor income is translating directly into consumption demand, amplifying the positive signal for corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and RSAFS changes yields a rank IC below 0.03 with 20-day SPX returns over any 2-year rolling window.
